question.text: In the context of anchor investor allocation in an IPO, what percentage of the QIB portion can be allocated to anchor investors?

answer.text: Up to 30% of the QIB portion
Up to 50% of the QIB portion
Up to 60% of the QIB portion
Up to 75% of the QIB portion

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI regulations allow up to 60% of the QIB portion to be allocated to anchor investors. This ensures sufficient institutional backing while leaving at least 40% of QIB portion for non-anchor QIBs. **The Trap:** 50% is a common wrong answer because candidates assume an equal split between anchor and non-anchor QIBs. The actual split favors anchor investors at 60:40. **Cross-Exam Context:** Anchor investor allocation is primarily tested in NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking). Other NISM exams rarely go into this level of detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember "60-40" — 60% for anchors, 40% for non-anchor QIBs. Within the anchor portion, further reservations apply for domestic MFs and insurance/pension funds.
